reasoning: Lead styphnate is the lead salt of styphnic acid (2,4,6-trinitroresorcinol), where lead coordinates with the oxygen atoms of the resorcinol ring. As an inorganic lead salt of an organic acid, it fits best under mixed metal/non-metal compounds; however, it could also be considered an organic lead salt, but its primary classification as an inorganic explosive primary initiator places it in mixed metal/non-metal compounds.
